evidence.quote: iPSCs were seeded onto a Matrigel-coated plate in E8 medium... containing 10 μM ROCK inhibitor... at a density of 15,000 cells/cm2... changing the E8 medium to E6 medium... after 24 h... switching to human endothelial serum free medium (hESFM)... supplemented with 10 nM bFGF... 10 μM all-trans retinoic acid (RA)... and 1% B27
19. Neal EH, Marinelli NA, Shi Y, et al. A simplified, fully defined differentiation scheme for producing blood–brain barrier endothelial cells from human iPSCs. Stem Cell Reports. 2019;12:1380–8.
HEK 293 cells were seeded at a density of 7×10^4 cell/cm2 in a 6-well plate... maintained in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM... supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin–streptomycin
transfected with 1 μg/well of the HA-tagged Fpn plasmid (Vector Builder, VB220407-1185gaa... using Lipofectamine™3000 Transfection Reagent (Invitrogen, L3000001)

Cells were exposed to apo- or holo-Tf... for 10 min... 100 μl Co-IP lysis buffer (20 mM Tris HCl, pH 8, 137 mM NaCl, 10% glycerol, 1% Triton x-100, and 2 mM EDTA)... Approximately 1 mg of protein was used for Co-IP using anti-HA magnetic beads (Thermo, 88837) or Protein G magnetic beads (Thermo, 10003D) complexed with antiHeph antibody (Santa Cruz, SC-365365)
Tf was co-immunoprecipitated with HA-Fpn... Tf was co-immunoprecipitated (Fig. 2B) further confirming that Fpn, Heph, and Tf complex together

PLA was performed using a Duolink assay kit (Sigma-Aldrich, DUO92013)... density of 15,000 cell/cm2... exposed to apoor holo-Tf... for 10 min... Primary antibodies used were the following:... Tf (ProteinTech, 66161-1... Tf (Abcam, ab82411... Fpn (gift from M. Knutson... and Heph (Santa Cruz, SC-365365
holo-Tf directly interacts with Fpn... apo-Tf directly interacts with Heph
co-incubated with 500 nM hepcidin (standard concentration in the literature... and varying concentrations of holo-Tf... for 10 min
Hepcidin interrupted the interaction between 0.25 μM holo-Tf and Fpn... physiological concentration of hepcidin [33], 25 nM, had no impact
12. Nemeth E, Tuttle MS, Powelson J, et al. Hepcidin regulates cellular iron efflux by binding to ferroportin and inducing its internalization. Science. 2004;306:2090–3.
co-incubated with 500 nM hepcidin and varying concentrations of apo-Tf... for 10 min
500 nM hepcidin did not interrupt the interaction between apo-Tf and Heph... No concentration of hepcidin was sufficient to alter the interaction
pretreatment with 50 μM PYR-41 for 30 min... 0.25 μM holo-Tf and 500 nM hepcidin for 10 min
this decreased interaction is prevented by PYR-41 pretreatment... indicates that hepcidin decreases the interaction between holo-Tf and Fpn due to the ability of hepcidin to rapidly internalize Fpn
15. De Domenico I, Ward DM, Langelier C, et al. The molecular mechanism of hepcidin-mediated ferroportin down-regulation. Mol Biol Cell. 2007;18:2569–78.

incubating with 200 μl digitonin buffer (20 mM Tris–HCl, 250 mM sucrose, 0.007% digitonin, 1× protease inhibitor cocktail)... spun at 1500×g for 10 min... spun again at 10,000×g for 10 min... stained for total protein content using Ponceau S
Hepcidin reduces membrane Fpn by nearly 50% in 5 min, whereas holo-Tf only reduces membrane Fpn by 20% after 60 min

loaded onto a 4-20% Criterion TGX Precast Protein Gel (Bio-Rad)... probed for Fpn... DMT1... Heph... TfR... Tf... HA tag... ubiquitin... or cyclophilin B... as a loading control... secondary antibody conjugated to HRP was used (1:5000, GE Amersham)... visualized using ECL reagents... on an Amersham Imager 600
Incubations with holo-Tf decreased Fpn protein levels by 50% at concentrations as low as 0.1 μM... whereas apo-Tf had no impact on Fpn

Statistical analyses were performed using Prism 9.2 software... Data from at least three independent biological replicates... mean ± standard error of the mean (SEM). One-way ANOVA with Tukey post-hoc analysis, two-way ANOVA with Sidak's post hoc analysis, or unpaired t-tests... p-value <0.05 was considered significant
